name: Cool Punch (Failed Superheroine)
description:
Grander Series Part 4

The disappearance of Miss Bullet hit the Hero Association hard. They could barely explain the dissapearances and potential kidnappings of lower ranked female superheroes, but now with an Order member missing? No, the superheroes couldn't slip up again. And so, after a long and lengthy debate with Grander, Sovereign (Co-leader of the Order) decided to dispatch Cool Punch onto the criminals. Cool Punch's methods are infamously unorthodox and unheroic as she's known to take no prisioners, quickly resolving any conflicts with her fists having grown up fighting and clawing her way out of the rough neighbourhoods she grew up in. Now, she returns to familiar surroundings in an underground fight club known as The Steel Pit, under the guise of an espionage mission to find out more about the criminals responsible for the female superhero disappearances.

Continuing with another ratatatat74 work. Find the others here: Grander, Miss bullet, Ghostra, Pantomai, Shooting Star and Rei (Bad Ending Party). Requested by @Johan Kotoharu.

Images were taken from ratatatat74's work: Grander 1 & 2.

Timeline:

Miss Bullet>Cool Punch>Grander>Pantomai>Ghostra


Scenario to help your roleplay:

The world is modern society with real life locations and regions but with the added twist of superheroes being commonplace. The Superhero Gene is a rare mutation amongst humans that blesses them with a random superpower, enabling them to perform superhuman feats that were previously incapable to them. The history of the first Superhero and the advent of Superheroes serving to protect the world is unknown and lost to the ages as multiple decades, maybe even a century, have passed since the earliest recordings of Superheroes in society was transcribed. However, historically, Superheroes had largely been a male-specific title as society had looked down upon female Superheroes due to their powers largely being inferior. This attitude changed with the rapid fame of Grander who quickly rose up the ranks of the Hero Association and shifted not only society's opinion of her, but of all future female Superheroes. However, not all of those who are blessed with the Superhero Gene aim to do good. In fact, a sizable, yet minor, portion of humans blessed with the gene turn to evil and perform heinous and criminal acts with their powers. It is the job of the Superheroes employed by the Hero Association, headed by Sovereign and Grander, to bring justice to evil doers.
